title: Project Standards Schema
description: Structured catalog of project-wide standards and guidelines for non-software projects. Adaptable to academic, creative, business, or personal projects.
$comment: This schema is intentionally open. Add sections that best represent your project's standards. The predefined sections are suggestions—rename or add as needed for your domain.

project_name.$comment: Examples: 'Master Thesis: AI Ethics', 'Acme Brand Redesign', 'Q4 Sales Strategy'.

project_type.description: Category of project to provide context for standards.

project_description.description: Brief explanation of the project's purpose and scope.
project_description.$comment: Provides context for why these standards exist and how they should be applied.

standards.description: Organized standard sections. Add sections relevant to your project domain.
standards.$comment: Predefined sections are suggestions. Add custom sections as needed: 'citation_format', 'brand_voice', 'legal_compliance', etc.

formatting.$comment: Document formatting rules. Examples: 'Use 12pt Times New Roman, double-spaced' (academic), 'All deliverables in PDF format with embedded fonts' (creative), 'Executive summaries max 1 page' (business).

style.$comment: Writing or visual style guidelines. Examples: 'Formal academic tone, third person' (academic), 'Brand voice: professional yet approachable' (creative), 'Data-driven language with clear metrics' (business).

citations.$comment: Reference and citation standards. Examples: 'APA 7th edition for all citations' (academic), 'Credit all stock images in appendix' (creative), 'Source all market data with date accessed' (business).

deliverables.$comment: Standards for project outputs. Examples: 'All chapters include abstract and keywords' (academic), 'Logo variations: full color, mono, reversed' (creative), 'All reports include executive summary' (business).

review_process.$comment: Quality assurance and review standards. Examples: 'Advisor review before each chapter submission' (academic), 'Client approval required at each milestone' (creative), 'Peer review for all financial projections' (business).

communication.$comment: Communication guidelines. Examples: 'Weekly progress updates to advisor' (academic), 'All client communication via project manager' (creative), 'Stakeholder updates every Monday' (business).

ethics_compliance.$comment: Ethical and compliance standards. Examples: 'IRB approval required for human subjects' (academic), 'GDPR compliant data handling' (creative), 'Financial projections must include assumptions' (business).

notes.$comment: Flexible section for information that doesn't fit standard categories.

exceptions.$comment: Document any approved deviations from standards.

pending_decisions.description: Standards that need clarification or approval.
pending_decisions.$comment: Track standards that are not yet finalized.

references.description: External resources or guides these standards are based on.
references.$comment: Examples: 'University Thesis Guidelines 2024', 'Client Brand Book v3', 'Industry Best Practices Report'.

standardList.description: List of standards for a given category.

standard.description: Single standard or guideline with identifier and timestamps.

id.description: Numeric identifier for the standard (sequential within section).
id.$comment: Stable identifier. Create once, never change. Allows referencing: 'Apply standard formatting #3'.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp when the standard was created.
created_at.$comment: Set once at creation. Never modify.

updated_at.description: UTC timestamp when the standard was last updated.
updated_at.$comment: Update when modifying description or scope.

description.description: Clear, actionable standard. Should be verifiable.
description.$comment: Write standards that can be objectively checked. Good: 'All figures must have captions and source citations'. Avoid: 'Make it look professional'.

rationale.$comment: Optional. Helps future team members understand the reasoning.

source.$comment: Examples: 'University guidelines', 'Client request', 'Industry best practice', 'Team decision'.

standard.$comment: Create standards that are: specific and verifiable, applicable throughout the project, not contradicting other standards. Apply YAGNI: only create standards for current needs, not hypothetical scenarios.
